title: Berlin Conference Pledges €1.3 Billion for Sudan; SAF Government Condemns Process as Sovereignty Violation
detail: The Third International Sudan Conference in Berlin concluded on April 15–16, 2026 with international donors pledging approximately €1.3 billion ($1.4 billion) in humanitarian aid, reconstruction assistance, and support for the civilian-led peace process. Germany pledged €230 million. The pledging conference, organized by the AU-UN-EU-Arab League-IGAD quintet, was attended by representatives of Sudanese civilian political blocs and humanitarian organizations but excluded both the SAF and RSF leadership by design. Sudan's SAF-aligned Port Sudan government, which had formally rejected the Berlin process, responded with a statement expressing 'profound surprise and condemnation,' characterizing the conference as a violation of Sudanese sovereignty and international law. The conference's stated goals included jump-starting the stalled UN humanitarian appeal (which had raised only 16% of its $2.9 billion target as of April 1) and building a civilian-inclusive political framework. Aid organizations welcomed the pledges but noted that conference pledges historically take months to materialize as actual disbursements and that immediate access challenges in conflict zones remain unresolved. The SAF government's formal rejection of the process raises acute questions about whether the pledged resources can be effectively channeled inside Sudan.

media.caption: Third International Sudan Conference in Berlin pledges €1.3 billion for humanitarian aid and peace process

title: Drone Strikes Kill at Least 44 Civilians in Central Darfur and West Kordofan
detail: Drone strikes on April 16, 2026 killed at least 44 civilians across Darfur and Kordofan. In Umm Dukhun district, Central Darfur, at least eight munitions were fired, killing at least 24 civilians and injuring approximately 20 others. In El Fula, capital of West Kordofan State, a separate drone strike hit the El Wahda market, killing nine civilians — including three women and six children. The attacks came one day after the Berlin International Conference concluded with €1.3 billion in pledges, underscoring the disconnect between international pledging events and the ongoing daily reality of aerial strikes against civilian populations across Sudan's conflict zones. The April 16 attacks continue a documented pattern of nearly 700 civilians killed in drone strikes across Darfur and Kordofan in the first three months of 2026 alone.

media.caption: Drone attacks kill at least 44 civilians in Central Darfur and West Kordofan on April 16, 2026

title: Sudan War Enters Fourth Year with Devastating Toll
detail: As the Sudan civil war marks its fourth year, new casualty figures reveal the devastating human cost of the conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and Rapid Support Forces (RSF). Over 150,000 people have been killed since fighting began in April 2023, while 14 million Sudanese have been displaced from their homes. The conflict has created one of the world's largest humanitarian crises, with widespread famine conditions and the collapse of basic services across much of the country.

media.caption: Sudan conflict enters its fourth year with massive humanitarian toll
